name: notes
problem_statement: Build a tiny notes service from a skeleton. notes.store.Store is a key-value store: put(key, value) saves and get(key) returns it. notes.service.NoteService wraps a Store: render(title, body) returns the string 'title: body'; add(title, body) must render the note, persist it through the Store under a string id counting up from '0', and return that id; get(note_id) returns the stored note for that id. Implement Store and NoteService so each piece works on its own and the pieces compose end-to-end.

base_files.notes/__init__.py:
from notes.service import NoteService
from notes.store import Store

__all__ = ["NoteService", "Store"]

test_files.tests/test_unit.py:
from notes.service import NoteService
from notes.store import Store


def test_store_put_get():
    store = Store()
    store.put("k", "v")
    assert store.get("k") == "v"


def test_store_overwrite():
    store = Store()
    store.put("k", "v1")
    store.put("k", "v2")
    assert store.get("k") == "v2"


def test_service_render():
    svc = NoteService(Store())
    assert svc.render("Hi", "there") == "Hi: there"
test_files.tests/test_integration.py:
from notes.service import NoteService
from notes.store import Store


def test_add_then_get_roundtrip():
    svc = NoteService(Store())
    note_id = svc.add("Hi", "there")
    assert note_id == "0"
    assert svc.get(note_id) == "Hi: there"


def test_persists_via_store():
    store = Store()
    svc = NoteService(store)
    note_id = svc.add("A", "b")
    assert store.get(note_id) == "A: b"
